module-pool-019.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Production incident triage for Module Pool follows a fixed playbook — ST22, SM50, SM12, SM13, SM21, ST05.

Used for:
- L2/L3 support tickets on frozen, dumping, or non-saving screens
- Root-cause analysis of LOOP AT CONTROL structure mismatches
- Bridging from symptom to fix without debugging in production

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- ST22 dumps (DYNPRO_FIELD_CONVERSION, GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED, CONNE_IMPORT_WRONG_OBJECT)
- SM50 work processes, SM12 locks, SM13 update tasks, SM21 system log
- ST05 SQL trace, SE51 to validate screen structures match work areas

Interview keyword: ST22 first, then SM12 and SM13 — never live-debug in production.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you use ST22 to diagnose a Module Pool short dump?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: ST22 mein main aaj ke dumps select karta hoon, relevant ek ko program name aur timestamp se identify karta hoon, aur detail open karta hoon. Dump exact error category, error message, program include, aur line number dikhata hai. Main SE38 mein us line pe navigate karke context samajhta hoon. Active Call Stack section full call chain dikhata hai. Dump ke time pe variable values bhi visible hote hain. Yeh mujhe enough information deta hai issue ko reproduce aur development mein fix karne ke liye bina kabhi production debug kiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In ST22 I select today's dumps, identify the relevant one by program name and timestamp, and open the detail. The dump shows the exact error category, error message, program include, and line number. I navigate to that line in SE38 to understand the context. The Active Call Stack section shows the full call chain. Variable values at the time of dump are also visible. This gives me enough information to reproduce and fix the issue in development without ever debugging production.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production dump: GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED at line 47 in ZPAY_MODULE_POOL. ST22 dump showed: field symbol <fs_item> not assigned before ASSIGN. Code pe gaya — READ TABLE ke baad sy-subrc check nahi kiya, seedha <fs_item> use kiya. Fix: IF sy-subrc = 0 guard add kiya before ASSIGN.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: ST22 mein dump open kiye bina seedha code debug karna — hit and miss approach. ST22 exact line aur variables batata hai — start here always. Time save hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" ST22 dump analysis → code fix example
" Dump: GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED at include ZPAY_MP_PAI, Line 47

" Before fix (causing dump)
FIELD-SYMBOLS: <fs_item> TYPE zgiss_item_s.
READ TABLE lt_items ASSIGNING <fs_item>
  WITH KEY matnr = lv_mat.
" Dump here if not found — <fs_item> not assigned
lv_qty = <fs_item>-menge. " Line 47 in dump

" After fix
IF sy-subrc = 0.           " Guard: check before use
  lv_qty = <fs_item>-menge.
ELSE.
  lv_qty = 0. " Default
ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you use SM50 and SM12 for Module Pool screen freeze issues?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SM50 saare active dialog work processes dikhata hai. Ek frozen module pool screen typically extended time ke liye Running state mein stuck work process se correspond karta hai. Main work process ko username aur program name se identify karta hoon, duration check karta hoon, aur cause investigate karta hoon — usually PAI mein ek long-running SELECT ya unoptimized LOOP. SM12 current locks dikhata hai — agar module pool PAI ne ENQUEUE lock acquire kiya but session DEQUEUE se pehle crash kar gaya, ek stale lock rehta hai. Main SM04 mein lock owner ke active session check karke stale locks identify karta hoon aur SM12 mein release karta hoon agar session no longer exist karta.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SM50 shows all active dialog work processes. A frozen module pool screen typically corresponds to a work process stuck in Running state for an extended time. I identify the work process by username and program name, check the duration, and investigate the cause — usually a long-running SELECT or unoptimized LOOP in PAI. SM12 shows current locks — if a module pool PAI acquired an ENQUEUE lock but the session crashed before DEQUEUE, a stale lock remains. I identify stale locks by checking the lock owner's active session in SM04 and release them in SM12 if the session no longer exists.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production call: user bol raha tha screen 10 minutes se respond nahi kar rahi. SM50 mein: his dialog WP Running, Time = 00:12:45, program = ZGOODSISS_MP. Root cause: PAI mein SELECT FROM MARC with no WHERE clause — fetching millions of records. Cancelled the WP, fixed the SELECT with proper WHERE, transport kiya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: SM50 mein Running process seedha cancel karna bina investigation ke — user ka kaam lost ho jaata hai. Pehle investigate karo — kya process legitimately long hai (e.g., large data batch)? Sirf stale/hung processes cancel karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SM50 investigation steps:
" 1. SM50 → Dialog work processes → Filter by Status = Running
" 2. Find WP with high Time and your program name
" 3. Check 'Reason' column — usually 'DB Wait' or 'Processing'
" 4. For DB Wait: slow SQL query — check ST05 for the query

" SM12 lock check steps:
" 1. SM12 → Enter * for all users
" 2. Look for locks with your module pool table
" 3. Check lock owner session in SM04
" 4. If session inactive → delete lock in SM12

" Fix: DEQUEUE missing in module pool PAI error handling
MODULE pai_0100 INPUT.
  CALL FUNCTION 'ENQUEUE_EZGISS'
    EXPORTING matnr = ls_item-matnr.
  PERFORM risky_operation.
  CALL FUNCTION 'DEQUEUE_EZGISS'  " Always dequeue!
    EXPORTING matnr = ls_item-matnr.
ENDMODULE.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle a DYNPRO_FIELD_CONVERSION dump in Module Pool?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: DYNPRO_FIELD_CONVERSION tab occur hota hai jab SE51 mein screen field definition aur corresponding ABAP variable ke beech type ya length mismatch hota hai. ST22 field name aur screen number dikhata hai. Main field ka ABAP Dictionary reference SE51 mein check karta hoon aur program mein variable declaration ke saath compare karta hoon. Common causes: DDIC structure change hui aur screen regenerate nahi hui, ya ABAP variable incorrectly typed tha. Fix: variable type ko match karne ke liye update karo, phir SE51 mein screen activate aur regenerate karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: DYNPRO_FIELD_CONVERSION occurs when there is a type or length mismatch between the screen field definition in SE51 and the corresponding ABAP variable. ST22 shows the field name and screen number. I check the field's ABAP Dictionary reference in SE51 and compare it to the variable declaration in the program. Common causes: the DDIC structure was changed and the screen was not regenerated, or the ABAP variable was incorrectly typed. Fix: update the variable type to match, then activate and regenerate the screen in SE51.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: DDIC structure ZGISS_ITEM_S mein MATNR field length change ki gaya 18 to 40 char. Screen 0200 regenerate karna bhool gaye. Next PAI run: DYNPRO_FIELD_CONVERSION dump. Fix: SE51 mein screen 0200 activate aur regenerate kiya. Dump gone. Lesson: DDIC change ke baad hamesha dependent screens regenerate karo.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: ABAP variable type change karna bina screen check kiye — ek end fix karna, dusra end mismatch rahta hai. Both sides check karo: ABAP variable type AND screen field definition.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" DYNPRO_FIELD_CONVERSION — diagnosis and fix
" ST22 shows: Field 'MATNR', Screen '0200', Type mismatch

" Check 1: SE51 → Program → Screen 0200 → Attributes
" Field MATNR: Format = CHAR, Length = 18 (old DDIC)
" After DDIC change: MATNR in structure = CHAR 40

" Fix: Regenerate screen in SE51
" SE51 → Screen 0200 → Activate (F3 or menu)
" Screen now reads DDIC with length 40 — mismatch resolved

" Also check ABAP variable:
" Wrong: DATA: lv_matnr TYPE c LENGTH 18.
" Correct: DATA: lv_matnr TYPE matnr. " Use DDIC type reference
" Using DDIC type means auto-sync when DDIC changes

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you reproduce a production Module Pool issue in development?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Main kabhi directly production mein debug nahi karta. Main ST22 dump details — exact program, line, variable values, aur error category — padhne se start karta hoon. Phir development mein main reported line pe ya pehle breakpoint set karta hoon aur production jaisa same data use karke exact input scenario replicate karta hoon. Agar production data needed hai, main data privacy guidelines follow karte hue development mein equivalent test data create karta hoon. Intermittent issues ke liye main development mein key PAI points pe custom log table mein WRITE TO ke through temporary logging add karta hoon, scenario reproduce karta hoon, aur exact failure cause karne wali condition narrow down karne ke liye log read karta hoon.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: I never debug directly in production. I start by reading ST22 dump details — exact program, line, variable values, and error category. Then in development I set a breakpoint at or before the reported line and replicate the exact input scenario using the same data as production. If production data is needed, I create equivalent test data in development following data privacy guidelines. For intermittent issues I add temporary logging via WRITE TO custom log table at key PAI points in development, reproduce the scenario, and read the log to narrow down the exact condition causing the failure.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Issue: Screen dump only happens when 48 or more rows in Table Control. Could not reproduce with 10 rows. Investigated: tc_items-lines variable — with 48 rows an internal table index exceeded bounds in PAI LOOP. Added temporary MESSAGE W in LOOP to show sy-tabix. Reproduced with 48 rows in development. Found: MODIFY lt_items FROM ls_item INDEX sy-tabix — sy-tabix exceeded table bounds. Fix: added bounds check.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Production mein BREAK-USER breakpoint lagana — aur phir bhool jaana remove karna. Next user jo woh program run karta hai debugger mein atak jaata hai. NEVER leave breakpoints in production. Always use BREAK-USER with your own user ID only in development.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Safe debugging in development only:
" 1. Set BREAK-USER breakpoint for your user
BREAK-USER sy-uname.  " Only stops for your user, safe in dev

" 2. Or conditional breakpoint:
IF sy-tabix = 48.  " Suspect row count
  BREAK-USER sy-uname.  " Stop here for investigation
ENDIF.

" 3. Temporary logging (dev only, remove before transport)
DATA: lv_log_msg TYPE string.
lv_log_msg = |PAI row { sy-tabix }: mat={ ls_item-matnr }|.
MESSAGE lv_log_msg TYPE 'W'.  " Remove before transport!

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you use SM13 for Module Pool data save issues?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SM13 update task monitor dikhata hai jahaan failed asynchronous database updates appear karte hain. Jab ek Module Pool program deferred writes ke liye CALL FUNCTION IN UPDATE TASK use karta hai, agar update task fail karta hai user error notification dekh sakta hai ya save successful appear karta hai but data missing hai. SM13 mein main program name aur user se filter karta hoon, failed update entry ke error details read karta hoon, aur short-term fix ke roop mein update restart kar sakta hoon. Root cause — usually update function module mein ek unhandled exception ya database error — phir development mein fix hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SM13 shows the update task monitor where failed asynchronous database updates appear. When a Module Pool program uses CALL FUNCTION IN UPDATE TASK for deferred writes, if the update task fails the user may see an error notification or the save appears to succeed but data is missing. In SM13 I filter by program name and user, read the error details of the failed update entry, and can restart the update as a short-term fix. The root cause — usually an unhandled exception or database error in the update function module — is then fixed in development.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Support ticket: user bol raha tha 'Save successful message aaya but material document nahi bana'. SM13 mein: ZGISS_UPDATE function module failed — error: 'Number range not defined for document type'. Number range configuration missing tha in production. SPRO se number range configure kiya, SM13 se failed update restart kiya — document created.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: SM13 check karna bhool jaana jab user kahe 'data save nahi hua'. Direct code mein jaana without checking SM13. SM13 often shows the root cause directly — configuration, auth, DB error — saving hours of code investigation.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SM13 investigation steps:
" 1. SM13 → Enter user name → Select date range
" 2. Look for 'Error' status entries
" 3. Select entry → Display → Read error message
" 4. Common SM13 errors for Module Pool:
"    - Number range not defined
"    - Authorization failure in update task
"    - Database table locked
"    - DB insert failure (duplicate key)

" Module Pool with UPDATE TASK (pattern that SM13 monitors):
CALL FUNCTION 'ZGISS_DO_SAVE'
  IN UPDATE TASK             " Deferred to update work process
  EXPORTING is_header = ls_header
             it_items  = lt_items.
COMMIT WORK.                 " Triggers the update task
" SM13 will show this function if it fails

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the difference between P1 and P2 incident handling for Module Pool?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: P1 incidents matlab complete business process stoppage — transaction ke saare users blocked hain bina koi workaround ke. Module pool P1 ke liye, main immediately issue ko development mein replicate karta hoon, root cause identify aur fix karta hoon, aur emergency transport process follow karta hoon jisme typically post-facto documentation ke saath change advisory board approval bypass required hota hai. Management ko 30 minutes pe communication. P2 matlab partial impact workaround available ke saath — same-day fix standard transport use karke, transport se pehle thorough testing.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: P1 incidents mean complete business process stoppage — all users of the transaction are blocked with no workaround. For module pool P1, I immediately replicate the issue in development, identify and fix the root cause, and follow the emergency transport process which typically requires change advisory board approval bypass with post-facto documentation. Communication every 30 minutes to management. P2 means partial impact with a workaround available — same-day fix using standard transport, thorough testing before transport.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Real P1: module pool T-code giving dump for ALL users after a Basis team transport accidentally overwrote a screen. Immediate action: identified wrong screen was transported (older version), raised emergency retransport of correct screen version. Fix deployed in 45 minutes. Root cause: missing transport dependency check by Basis.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: P1 incident pe thorough testing karne mein time lagaana — agar business is blocked, 80% confident fix with fast deployment better hai. Perfect solution baad mein refine kar sakte hain. Speed matters in P1.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" P1 Incident response checklist:
" [ ] 1. Verify incident scope (all users? specific users?)
" [ ] 2. ST22 — identify dump, read error
" [ ] 3. Provide workaround (use alternate T-code?)
" [ ] 4. Replicate in development
" [ ] 5. Fix and unit test
" [ ] 6. Emergency CAB approval for transport
" [ ] 7. Transport to production
" [ ] 8. Verify fix with 2-3 test users before announcing
" [ ] 9. Monitor SM50/ST22 for 30 min post-fix
" [ ] 10. Root cause analysis document (RCA)

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle a Module Pool screen that shows stale data after navigation?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Screen navigation pe stale data tab occur hoti hai jab screens ke beech shared global variables ek screen ke PAI ke through modified hote hain but doosri screen ke PBO mein refreshed nahi. Fix yeh hai ki affected screen ke PBO mein proper initialization logic add karo — ya toh variables populate karne se pehle clear karo, ya preserved key use karke database se re-read karo. Main navigation ke across record key retain karne ke liye GET PARAMETER ID use karta hoon, phir PBO mein current database data ke saath screen populate karne ke liye fresh SELECT karta hoon.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Stale data on screen navigation occurs when global variables shared between screens are modified by one screen's PAI but not refreshed in another screen's PBO. The fix is to add proper initialization logic in the PBO of the affected screen — either clearing variables before populating them, or re-reading from the database using the preserved key. I use GET PARAMETER ID to retain the record key across navigation, then in PBO do a fresh SELECT to populate the screen with current database data.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Issue: vendor module pool, screen 100 showing vendor number entered on previous run. PBO not clearing ls_header before displaying. Fix: in PBO 0100, added CLEAR ls_header before any initialization. If returning from screen 200 with saved data, read from Z custom table using header key. Fresh data every time screen 100 displays.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: PBO mein sirf SET PF-STATUS aur SET TITLEBAR — koi data initialization nahi. Sochna ki data automatically correct rahega. Hamesha PBO mein clear aur re-initialize karo display data.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Fix for stale data — PBO with proper initialization
MODULE pbo_0100 OUTPUT.
  SET PF-STATUS 'ZSTATUS'.
  SET TITLEBAR 'ZTITLE'.
  " Clear before populate — no stale data
  CLEAR ls_header.
  " If we have a saved key, read fresh from DB
  IF gv_saved_key IS NOT INITIAL.
    SELECT SINGLE * FROM zgiss_header
      WHERE zdoc = gv_saved_key
      INTO @ls_header.
  ELSE.
    " New entry — set defaults
    GET PARAMETER ID 'WRK' FIELD ls_header-werks. " Plant default via SET/GET PARAMETER ID
    ls_header-erdat = sy-datum.
  ENDIF.
ENDMODULE.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you monitor a Module Pool transaction post go-live?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Post go-live monitoring mein module pool program se related new dumps ke liye ST22 ka daily check, failed update tasks ke liye SM13, aur transaction success rates aur error patterns ke liye custom audit log table include hote hain. Main ek background job use karke simple daily report set up karta hoon jo log table read karta hai aur error summaries email karta hai. Pehle two weeks ke liye main key users ke saath daily 15-minute check-in calls bhi rakhta hoon usability issues early catch karne ke liye. 2% ke upar koi bhi error rate immediate root cause investigation trigger karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Post go-live monitoring includes daily checks of ST22 for new dumps related to the module pool program, SM13 for failed update tasks, and the custom audit log table for transaction success rates and error patterns. I set up a simple daily report using a background job that reads the log table and emails error summaries. For the first two weeks I also hold daily 15-minute check-in calls with key users to catch usability issues early. Any error rate above 2% triggers immediate root cause investigation.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Post go-live week 1: ZGISS_LOG se pata chala 8% transactions failing — all with same error: batch not found. Investigation: users entering batch in lowercase, validation doing exact match (case-sensitive). Fix: TRANSLATE ls_item-charg TO UPPER CASE before validation. Error rate dropped to 0.3%. Log table ne iss pattern ko identify karne mein help kiya — impossible to catch in testing.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Go-live ke baad monitoring band kar dena — 'transaction live hai, kaam ho gaya'. Production mein real user behavior testing mein nahi aata. First 4 weeks monitoring mandatory hai naye module pool ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Background job for daily monitoring — simple SELECT
REPORT zgiss_daily_monitor.
DATA: lv_date   TYPE sy-datum,
      lv_total  TYPE i,
      lv_errors TYPE i.
lv_date = sy-datum - 1.  " Yesterday
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM zgiss_log
  WHERE zdate = @lv_date INTO @lv_total.
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM zgiss_log
  WHERE zdate = @lv_date AND zstatus = 'E'
  INTO @lv_errors.
IF lv_total > 0.
  DATA(lv_error_pct) = ( lv_errors * 100 ) / lv_total.
  IF lv_error_pct > 2.  " Alert threshold
    " Send email to support team
  ENDIF.
ENDIF.
